claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 19 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 19 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 19 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 19 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess this, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances and your children.

Let me start with the first question:

**Do you have any children that you're claiming Child Benefit for, and if so, what are their names?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to claim Child Benefit for my child. Their name is Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have a child named Alex that you'd like to claim Child Benefit for.

To help me assess your eligibility, I need to understand a bit more about Alex's situation.

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good, thank you. That's helpful to know.

Now I need to check a few more details about Alex to ensure they meet the eligibility criteria.

**How old is Alex?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I need to understand a bit more about Alex's circumstances.

**Is Alex in full-time education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is in full-time education. They're studying for their A-levels.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. That's important information.

Before I can make a final decision, I need to check the specific eligibility rules to ensure I'm applying them correctly. Let me do that now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! I've now reviewed the eligibility rules. Based on the information you've provided, I can now make my assessment.

According to the UK Child Benefit eligibility rules, you can qualify for Child Benefit for a child aged 16 or over if they are under 20 and staying in approved education or training. Alex is 19 years old and in full-time education (studying for A-levels), which counts as approved education.

Since Alex:
- Lives with you
- Is 19 years old (under 20)
- Is in full-time approved education (A-levels)

You meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.** 

To summarize why:
- Alex is 19 years old (under the age limit of 20)
- Alex is in full-time approved education (A-levels)
- Alex lives with you

This means you can claim Child Benefit for Alex. If you have any other children you'd like to inquire about, I'd be happy to help assess their eligibility as well.

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit because: (1) Alex is under 20 years old (currently 19); (2) Alex is in approved education or training (studying for A-levels, which is full-time education); (3) Alex lives with the claimant. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex. Alex meets all the necessary criteria: being under 20 years old, remaining in full-time approved education (A-levels), and living with the claimant.">

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit because: (1) Alex is under 20 years old (currently 19); (2) Alex is in approved education or training (studying for A-levels, which is full-time education); (3) Alex lives with the claimant. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex. Alex meets all the necessary criteria: being under 20 years old, remaining in full-time approved education (A-levels), and living with the claimant.">
